source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
title: Ensure That Sinks Are Configured for All Log Entries

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure That Sinks Are Configured for All Log Entries
input_row.description: It is recommended to create a sink that will export copies of all the log entries. This can help aggregate logs from multiple projects and export them to a Security Information and Event Management (SIEM).
input_row.rationale: Log entries are held in Cloud Logging. To aggregate logs, export them to a SIEM. To keep them longer, it is recommended to set up a log sink. Exporting involves writing a filter that selects the log entries to export, and choosing a destination in Cloud Storage, BigQuery, or Cloud Pub/Sub. The filter and destination are held in an object called a sink. To ensure all log entries are exported to sinks, ensure that there is no filter configured for a sink. Sinks can be created in projects, organizations, folders, and billing accounts. Impact: There are no costs or limitations in Cloud Logging for exporting logs, but the export destinations charge for storing or transmitting the log data.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Logs Router by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/router. 2. For every sink, click the 3-dot button for Menu options and select View sink details. 3. Ensure there is at least one sink with an empty Inclusion filter. 4. Additionally, ensure that the resource configured as Destination exists. From Google Cloud CLI 1. Ensure that a sink with an empty filter exists. List the sinks for the project, folder or organization. If sinks are configured at a folder or organization level, they do not need to be configured for each project: gcloud logging sinks list --folder=FOLDER_ID | --organization=ORGANIZATION_ID | --project=PROJECT_ID The output should list at least one sink with an empty filter.  2. Additionally, ensure that the resource configured as Destination exists. See https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/beta/logging/sinks/list for more information.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. Go to Logs Router by visiting https://console.cloud.google.com/logs/router. 2. Click on the arrow symbol with CREATE SINK text. 3. Fill out the fields for Sink details. 4. Choose Cloud Logging bucket in the Select sink destination drop down menu. 5. Choose a log bucket in the next drop down menu. 6. If an inclusion filter is not provided for this sink, all ingested logs will be routed to the destination provided above. This may result in higher than expected resource usage. 7. Click Create Sink. For more information, see https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/export/configure_export_v2#dest-create. From Google Cloud CLI To create a sink to export all log entries in a Google Cloud Storage bucket: gcloud logging sinks create <sink-name> storage.googleapis.com/DESTINATION_BUCKET_NAME Sinks can be created for a folder or organization, which will include all projects. gcloud logging sinks create <sink-name> storage.googleapis.com/DESTINATION_BUCKET_NAME --include-children -- folder=FOLDER_ID | --organization=ORGANIZATION_ID Note: 1. A sink created by the command-line above will export logs in storage buckets. However, sinks can be configured to export logs into BigQuery, or Cloud Pub/Sub, or Custom Destination. 2. While creating a sink, the sink option --log-filter is not used to ensure the sink exports all log entries. 3. A sink can be created at a folder or organization level that collects the logs of all the projects underneath bypassing the option --include-children in the gcloud command. Default Value: By default, there are no sinks configured. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/reference/tools/gcloud-logging 2. https://cloud.google.com/logging/quotas 3. https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/routing/overview 4. https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/export/using_exported_logs 5. https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/export/configure_export_v2 6. https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/export/aggregated_exports 7. https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/beta/logging/sinks/list Additional Information: For Command-Line Audit and Remediation, the sink destination of type Cloud Storage Bucket is considered. However, the destination could be configured to Cloud Storage Bucket or BigQuery or Cloud Pub\Sub or Custom Destination. Command Line Interface commands would change accordingly.
input_row.impact: There are no costs or limitations in Cloud Logging for exporting logs, but the export destinations charge for storing or transmitting the log data.
input_row.references: 1. https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/reference/tools/gcloud-logging 2. https://cloud.google.com/logging/quotas 3. https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/routing/overview 4. https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/export/using_exported_logs 5. https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/export/configure_export_v2 6. https://cloud.google.com/logging/docs/export/aggregated_exports 7. https://cloud.google.com/sdk/gcloud/reference/beta/logging/sinks/list Additional Information: For Command-Line Audit and Remediation, the sink destination of type Cloud Storage Bucket is considered. However, the destination could be configured to Cloud Storage Bucket or BigQuery or Cloud Pub\Sub or Custom Destination. Command Line Interface commands would change accordingly.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The audit approach for this control is Automated because it leverages GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK to verify the configuration of log sinks. The control is designed to verify that log entries are being properly exported without the use of filters, ensuring that all log entries are captured and sent to designated destinations, such as Cloud Storage, BigQuery, or Cloud Pub/Sub.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_logging_sinks_all_entries_exported

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

- **GCP CLI Command:** Use the `gcloud logging sinks list` command to enumerate all configured sinks at the project, folder, or organization level. This ensures that sinks are correctly set up without filters that would limit the logs being exported.
  
  - For projects: `gcloud logging sinks list --project=PROJECT_ID`
  - For folders: `gcloud logging sinks list --folder=FOLDER_ID`
  - For organizations: `gcloud logging sinks list --organization=ORGANIZATION_ID`
  
- **Specific Checks:**
  - Enumerate the list of sinks and verify that there is at least one sink with an empty `filter` field. This ensures that all log entries are being exported.
  - Check that each sink has a valid destination configured. Confirm that the destination is active and accessible (e.g., a valid Cloud Storage bucket).
  
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - **Pass:** At least one sink exists with no filter, ensuring all log entries are being exported, and the specified destination is accessible and correctly configured.
  - **Fail:** No sinks exist, or sinks all have filters applied that limit the logs being exported, or the destination does not exist or is inaccessible.

Using these commands and checks allows for the automated verification of the control, aligning with industry best practices for CSPM tools.
step2_review:
---

### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: **AGREE**

- **Manual vs Automated:** The classification as Automated is correct. This control can be fully assessed using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK (`gcloud`). No manual review is required if the automation is implemented properly.
- **GCP SDK Capabilities:** The `gcloud logging sinks list` command supports enumerating sinks at the project, folder, and organization levels. The REST API (`logging.sinks.list`) and client libraries also support these queries.
- **Other Tools:** The assessment does not require CloudFormation (AWS-specific) or GCP Config (Config Validator could be used, but direct API/CLI is sufficient and more direct).

---

### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: **Score: 8/10**

- **Automation Details:**  
  - The use of `gcloud logging sinks list` at all resource levels is correct.
  - Checking for an empty `filter` field is the right approach to ensure all log entries are exported.
  - Verifying the destination is valid is necessary, but the method for checking if the destination is "active and accessible" is not detailed. This is a complex area (e.g., permissions, bucket existence, Pub/Sub topic existence, etc.) and is not fully covered in the assessment.
- **gcloud Commands:**  
  - The commands provided are syntactically correct.
- **Program Name:**  
  - `gcp_logging_sinks_all_entries_exported` is descriptive but could be more explicit (see Improvements).
- **Completeness:**  
  - The assessment does not mention checking for log sinks at all three levels (project, folder, organization) in aggregate, which is important for organizations using hierarchical logging.
  - It does not mention checking for log sink **writer identity** permissions on the destination, which is a common misconfiguration.
  - It does not mention **log exclusions** (which could exist separately from sink filters and affect log completeness).

---

### 3. IMPROVEMENTS

- **Destination Validation:**  
  - Expand on how to validate that the destination is "active and accessible." For example:
    - For Cloud Storage: Use `gsutil ls gs://BUCKET_NAME` or check via `gcloud storage buckets describe`.
    - For BigQuery: Use `bq show --dataset_id`.
    - For Pub/Sub: Use `gcloud pubsub topics describe`.
  - Check that the **sink service account** (writer identity) has the appropriate permissions on the destination.
- **Log Exclusions:**  
  - Consider that log exclusions may exist independently of sink filters and could prevent some logs from being exported. These should be checked via `gcloud logging exclusions list`.
- **Coverage:**  
  - Ensure that all levels (project, folder, organization) are checked, and that there is at least one "all logs" sink at the appropriate level.
- **Program Name:**  
  - Suggest: `gcp_logging_sink_unfiltered_export` or `gcp_logging_sink_all_logs_no_filter`
  - Consider including "no_filter" or "all_logs" in the name for clarity.
- **Alternative Methods:**  
  - The REST API (`logging.sinks.list`) and client libraries can be used for automation at scale.
  - For large organizations, consider using Asset Inventory (`gcloud asset search-all-resources --asset-types=logging.googleapis.com/LogSink`) to enumerate sinks across all projects/folders/orgs.

---

### 4. CONFIDENCE: **HIGH**

- The approach is fundamentally sound and leverages the correct GCP APIs and CLI commands.
- The technical details are mostly accurate, with minor gaps around destination validation and log exclusions.

---

### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: **Modify**

- **Keep the Automated classification.**
- **Modify the automation details** to:
  - Explicitly describe how to validate destination accessibility and permissions.
  - Include checks for log exclusions that may affect log completeness.
  - Ensure coverage at all hierarchy levels (project, folder, org).
  - Use a more descriptive program name, e.g., `gcp_logging_sink_all_logs_no_filter`.
- **Summary:** The assessment is strong, but should be updated to address the above technical gaps for completeness and best practice alignment.

---

**Example Improved Automation Details:**

- Enumerate sinks at project, folder, and org levels using `gcloud logging sinks list`.
- For each sink:
  - Ensure the `filter` field is empty (all logs exported).
  - Verify the destination exists and is accessible (using `gsutil`, `bq`, or `gcloud pubsub` as appropriate).
  - Check that the sink’s writer identity has the necessary permissions on the destination.
- Enumerate log exclusions (`gcloud logging exclusions list`) and ensure no exclusions are present that would prevent all logs from being exported.
- Pass if at least one unfiltered sink exists with a valid, accessible destination and no relevant exclusions.

---

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that this control can be fully audited using GCP APIs and the Google Cloud SDK. The required properties (sink existence, filter field, and destination) are all exposed via the `gcloud logging sinks list` command and the Logging API. No manual inspection is needed, as all compliance criteria can be programmatically validated, including verifying that at least one sink exists with an empty filter and a valid destination.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
gcp_logging_sink_all_entries_no_filter_and_valid_destination

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- GCP Resource/Service: Cloud Logging Sinks
- API/CLI Method:  
  - Google Cloud SDK: `gcloud logging sinks list` (with `--project`, `--folder`, or `--organization` as appropriate)
  - Logging API: `logging.sinks.list`
- Properties to Check:  
  - At least one sink exists with an empty `filter` field (i.e., all log entries are exported)
  - The `destination` field points to an existing and accessible resource (Cloud Storage bucket, BigQuery dataset, or Pub/Sub topic)
- Pass Criteria:  
  - There is at least one sink with an empty `filter` field
  - The destination resource exists and is accessible (e.g., bucket exists, Pub/Sub topic exists, BigQuery dataset exists)
- Fail Criteria:  
  - No sink exists with an empty `filter` field  
  - The destination resource does not exist or is not accessible

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
Both AI opinions agreed on the automated approach. The second opinion suggested a more explicit program name and highlighted the need to check destination existence/accessibility, which is now incorporated in the final program name and automation details.

**SOURCE:** CIS GOOGLE CLOUD PLATFORM FOUNDATION BENCHMARK V4.0.0
